import re

text = open('/mnt/data/file-1sQN5CLVjBmb6ravrKXide-Q017.txt','r',encoding='utf-8').read()
len(text)

# Helper to find all substring occurrences including overlapping?
def find_all_occurrences(s, sub):
    indices = []
    start = 0
    while True:
        idx = s.find(sub, start)
        if idx == -1:
            break
        indices.append(idx)
        start = idx + 1
    return indices

# 1) First quoted block: get content between the first set of " " quotes
quote_pattern = r'\"([^\"]+)\"'
matches = list(re.finditer(quote_pattern, text))
len(matches), [(m.start(), m.end(), m.group(1)[:30]) for m in matches[:5]]

first_quote = matches[0]
first_content = first_quote.group(1)
first_content

def segment_spans_for_star_delimited_quote(qmatch):
    content = qmatch.group(1)
    base = qmatch.start() + 1  # exclude opening quote
    # split by ' * ' preserving segments; we will not strip spaces
    parts = content.split(' * ')
    spans = []
    offset = 0
    for i, part in enumerate(parts):
        # find part at or after offset to handle duplicates
        idx = content.find(part, offset)
        if idx == -1:
            raise ValueError("Part not found")
        start = base + idx
        end = start + len(part)
        spans.append((part, start, end))
        offset = idx + len(part)
        # Skip over ' * ' after part for next search
    return spans

first_spans = segment_spans_for_star_delimited_quote(matches[0])
first_spans

second_quote = matches[1]
second_content = second_quote.group(1)
second_spans = segment_spans_for_star_delimited_quote(second_quote)
second_content, second_spans[:5]

third_phrase = 'قال تعالى: إِنَّ شَجَرَتَ الزَّقُّومِ * طَعَامُ الْأَثِيمِ * كَالْمُهْلِ يَغْلِي فِي الْبُطُونِ * كَغَلْيِ الْحَمِيمِ'
idx = text.find(third_phrase)
idx, text[idx:idx+len(third_phrase)+30]

start_idx = idx + len('قال تعالى: ')
# Find the end before ' الدخان/'
end_marker = ' الدخان/'
end_idx = text.find(end_marker, start_idx)
verse_block = text[start_idx:end_idx]
verse_block

third_spans = []
parts = verse_block.split(' * ')
base = start_idx
offset = 0
for part in parts:
    local_idx = verse_block.find(part, offset)
    start = base + local_idx
    end = start + len(part)
    third_spans.append((part, start, end))
    offset = local_idx + len(part)
third_spans

bracket_open = '﴿'
bracket_close = '﴾'
# Find all bracketed segments
bracket_spans = []
start = 0
while True:
    i = text.find(bracket_open, start)
    if i == -1:
        break
    j = text.find(bracket_close, i+1)
    if j == -1:
        break
    content_inside = text[i+1:j]
    bracket_spans.append((content_inside, i+1, j))  # exclude brackets from spans
    start = j+1
bracket_spans

occurs = find_all_occurrences(text, 'كوني')
contexts = [(i, text[max(0,i-20):i+50]) for i in occurs]
occurs, contexts[:5]
find_all_occurrences(text, 'بَرْداً')

i = 4555
print(text[i-40:i+60])

phrase = 'كُوني بَرْداً وسلاماً على إبراهيم'
start_idx_phrase = text.find(phrase)
start_idx_phrase, text[start_idx_phrase:start_idx_phrase+len(phrase)]

end_idx_phrase = start_idx_phrase + len(phrase)
end_idx_phrase

# Build the citations list programmatically
citations = []

def add_citation(span_start, span_end, sura_name, sura_number, aya_number, corrected_text, original_text=None):
    if original_text is None:
        original_text = text[span_start:span_end]
    citations.append({
        "span_start": span_start,
        "span_end": span_end,
        "span_text": original_text,
        "citation_type": "Aya",
        "is_valid": True,
        "aya_reference": {
            "sura_name": sura_name,
            "sura_number": sura_number,
            "aya_number": aya_number
        },
        "hadeeth_reference": {},
        "corrected_text": corrected_text,
        "completely_invalid": False
    })

# For the first quote segments (44:43-46)
mapping1 = [
    (first_spans[0], "الدخان", 44, 43, "إِنَّ شَجَرَتَ الزَّقُّومِ"),
    (first_spans[1], "الدخان", 44, 44, "طَعَامُ الْأَثِيمِ"),
    (first_spans[2], "الدخان", 44, 45, "كَالْمُهْلِ يَغْلِي فِي الْبُطُونِ"),
    (first_spans[3], "الدخان", 44, 46, "كَغَلْيِ الْحَمِيمِ"),
]
for (part, start, end), sname, snum, anum, corr in mapping1:
    add_citation(start, end, sname, snum, anum, corr, original_text=part)

# For the second quote segments (37:62-65) with corrected forms
mapping2 = [
    (second_spans[0], "الصافات", 37, 62, "أَذَٰلِكَ خَيْرٌ نُزُلًا أَمْ شَجَرَةُ الزَّقُّومِ"),
    (second_spans[1], "الصافات", 37, 63, "إِنَّا جَعَلْنَاهَا فِتْنَةً لِلظَّالِمِينَ"),
    (second_spans[2], "الصافات", 37, 64, "إِنَّهَا شَجَرَةٌ تَخْرُجُ فِي أَصْلِ الْجَحِيمِ"),
    (second_spans[3], "الصافات", 37, 65, "طَلْعُهَا كَأَنَّهُ رُءُوسُ الشَّيَاطِينِ"),
]
for (part, start, end), sname, snum, anum, corr in mapping2:
    add_citation(start, end, sname, snum, anum, corr, original_text=part)

# For the third mention (again 44:43-46)
mapping3 = [
    (third_spans[0], "الدخان", 44, 43, "إِنَّ شَجَرَتَ الزَّقُّومِ"),
    (third_spans[1], "الدخان", 44, 44, "طَعَامُ الْأَثِيمِ"),
    (third_spans[2], "الدخان", 44, 45, "كَالْمُهْلِ يَغْلِي فِي الْبُطُونِ"),
    (third_spans[3], "الدخان", 44, 46, "كَغَلْيِ الْحَمِيمِ"),
]
for (part, start, end), sname, snum, anum, corr in mapping3:
    add_citation(start, end, sname, snum, anum, corr, original_text=part)

# For the bracketed partial "شَجَرَةُ الزَّقُّومِ"

br_content, br_start, br_end = bracket_spans[0]
add_citation(br_start, br_end, "الصافات", 37, 62, "شَجَرَةُ الزَّقُّومِ", original_text=br_content)

# For the phrase "كُوني بَرْداً وسلاماً على إبراهيم" referencing 21:69 (partial)
add_citation(start_idx_phrase, end_idx_phrase, "الأنبياء", 21, 69, "كُونِي بَرْدًا وَسَلَامًا عَلَىٰ إِبْرَاهِيمَ")

len(citations), citations[0]

# Validation: for each citation ensure the substring matches span_text:
all_ok = True
bad = []
for c in citations:
    ss = c["span_start"]; ee = c["span_end"]; st = c["span_text"]
    if text[ss:ee] != st:
        all_ok = False
        bad.append((c, text[ss:ee]))
all_ok, len(bad), bad[:1]
print(json.dumps(output, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2))

citations.description: List of citations with details, validation, references, and per-citation corrections.

span_start.description: Inclusive start character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_end.description: Exclusive end character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_text.description: The text content of the citation span.

citation_type.description: Type of the citation: either Aya (Quran) or Hadeeth (Prophetic tradition).

is_valid.description: True if the cited Aya or Hadeeth is recognized and valid, false otherwise.

aya_reference.description: Quranic reference details (required if citation_type is Aya; empty object if not applicable).

sura_name.description: Name of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

sura_number.description: Number of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

aya_number.description: Number of the aya (verse) in the Quran.

hadeeth_reference.description: Hadeeth reference details (required if citation_type is Hadeeth; empty object if not applicable).

collection_name.description: Name of the Hadeeth collection in english (e.g. Sahih Bukhari, Muslim).

hadith_number.description: Hadith number within the specified book.

corrected_text.description: The corrected version of the citation text (or the text itself if it was correct in the first place) (e.g. if there are certain words that were invalid this version must have this corrected), or 'خطأ' for completely invalid citations (i.e. no close enough citation can be found to correct the text). If there are close texts in Quran or Hadith - but too far from the citation, this must be considered 'خطأ' and completely_invalid must be true.

completely_invalid.description: Set to true if the citation is completely invalid i.e. there can't be a close enough text to correct it, (corrected_text must be 'خطأ').
